message:
chore(beads): close stream RDB subbeads proven by live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream

- qi6z (type-21 decoder): reverse direction of m17o test
- 6zk9 (type-21 encoder): forward direction of m17o test
- hjub (type-15 entries decoder): covered by fr-persist::rdb_stream
- s5su (umbrella stream interchange): bidirectional round-trip proven

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-29c7","title":"Add RESP parser fuzz test for crash resilience on malformed input","description":"# RESP Parser Fuzz Test

## What
A property-based test that feeds random/malformed bytes to the RESP parser and verifies it never panics, only returns Ok or Err.

## Why
The RESP parser is the first code that touches untrusted network input. A panic crashes the entire server. Property-based testing (proptest) is the practical approach since cargo-fuzz requires nightly and special setup.

## Implementation
1. Add proptest as a dev-dependency to fr-protocol
2. New test in crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs test module:
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn parse_frame_never_panics(data: Vec<u8>) {
           let _ = parse_frame(&data); // Ok or Err, never panic
       }
   }
   ```
3. Also test parse_frame_with_config with various ParserConfig settings
4. Also test with structured semi-valid input: valid RESP prefix byte + random payload
5. Run for at least 10,000 cases (proptest default is 256, increase with PROPTEST_CASES env)
6. Fix any panics found

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-protocol -- parse_frame_never_panics` passes
- Run with PROPTEST_CASES=100000 in CI

## Files
- crates/fr-protocol/Cargo.toml (add proptest dev-dependency)
- crates/fr-protocol/src/lib.rs (add proptest tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:50.074946085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736277717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:17:52.736003488Z","close_reason":"Added 4 proptest fuzz tests to fr-protocol: parse_frame_never_panics, parse_frame_with_config_never_panics, parse_frame_with_tight_limits_never_panics, parse_frame_with_resp_prefix_never_panics. Each runs 10,000 random input cases. All pass — zero panics found. Added proptest dev-dependency.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-2auy","title":"Add minimal client session context to Store for CLIENT stubs in dispatch_argv","description":"# Add minimal client context to Store for CLIENT command in dispatch_argv

## Problem
CLIENT ID/GETNAME/SETNAME/LIST/INFO in fr-command return hardcoded values (id=1, name=nil, etc.) because they don't have access to the ClientSession. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") get 1 instead of their real client ID.

## Design
Add a \"current client context\" to Store that the runtime populates before dispatching:
```rust
pub struct DispatchClientContext {
    pub client_id: u64,
    pub client_name: Option<Vec<u8>>,
    pub db_index: usize,
    pub flags: String,   // e.g., \"N\" for normal
    pub peer_addr: String,
}
```

The runtime already swaps sessions before dispatch — it can populate this context at the same time.

## Implementation
1. Add pub dispatch_client_ctx: DispatchClientContext to Store
2. In Runtime::execute_frame (before dispatch_argv call), populate store.dispatch_client_ctx from current session
3. Update fr-command's client_cmd to read from store.dispatch_client_ctx:
   - CLIENT ID: return ctx.client_id
   - CLIENT GETNAME: return ctx.client_name
   - CLIENT SETNAME: update ctx.client_name (runtime will read it back)
   - CLIENT LIST/INFO: format using ctx fields
4. After dispatch_argv returns, runtime reads back any changes (e.g., SETNAME)

## Scope limitation
CLIENT LIST in Redis returns ALL connected clients. Through dispatch_argv, we can only report the current client. This is acceptable for Lua scripts (they typically only need their own info). The runtime's CLIENT LIST already returns all clients.

## Depends on
- No hard dependencies, but nice to land after the counter foundation

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"CLIENT\", \"ID\") and gets a real ID (not 1)
- Unit test: populate context, verify CLIENT GETNAME returns it

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (DispatchClientContext struct, Store field)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (client_cmd reads context)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (populate context before dispatch)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:41.559944609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359816290Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:26:45.359474203Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-2di1","title":"[HIGH] CONFIG GET/SET/REWRITE parity: port/dir/maxmemory defaults, multi-pattern order, case-insensitive echo, upstream error wording","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_config_matches_runtime (29 divergences): (a) CONFIG GET returns ephemeral values (port, dir) that differ between our fixture-pinned defaults and the upstream test-spawned instance; needs comparator ignore-list or harness-derived expected values. (b) CONFIG GET on multi-pattern invocations returns keys in different order — add comparator canonicalization like HGETALL. (c) CONFIG GET is case-insensitive on the reply key name — upstream returns the requested casing verbatim. (d) CONFIG SET unknown-parameter / non-integer / negative-integer / policy-invalid error wording all differ from upstream's 'ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument ...)' template. (e) CONFIG REWRITE returns OK where upstream returns 'ERR The server is running without a config file'. (f) hash-max-listpack-entries default 128 vs upstream 512; proto-max-bulk-len default 512000000 vs upstream 536870912. (g) active-defrag-enabled accepted silently where upstream rejects (not supported). (h) notify-keyspace-events canonicalization: ours echoes as-written, upstream normalizes to AKE order. Fix in fr-command CONFIG SET/GET error wording + fr-config defaults + harness comparator.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T08:45:44.648576531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T23:10:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-24T23:10:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"CONFIG GET/SET/REWRITE wording + fixture updates landed; core_config 100% STRICT."}

-{"id":"frankenredis-hjub","title":"s5su part c: stream decoder for RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS (15) — entries only","description":"Extend fr-persist::decode_rdb to accept upstream type-15 stream records and reconstruct RdbValue::Stream(entries, watermark, []). No consumer groups yet — blank Vec. Uses the listpack decoder from s5su-b.

Scope:
  * Read radix tree pair count, iterate each (stream-id, listpack) pair.
  * Decode listpack entries into (ms, seq, fields) tuples.
  * Populate watermark from stream metadata (last_id).
  * Leave consumer_groups empty (covered by s5su-d).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:58.348959392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T16:03:11.285273263Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

No consumer groups yet — blank Vec. Uses the listpack decoder from s5su-b.

Scope:
  * Read radix tree pair count, iterate each (stream-id, listpack) pair.
  * Decode listpack entries into (ms, seq, fields) tuples.
  * Populate watermark from stream metadata (last_id).
  * Leave consumer_groups empty (covered by s5su-d).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:58.348959392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T23:12:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"closed_at":"2026-04-24T23:12:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Decoder path for type-15 STREAM_LISTPACKS exists in fr-persist::rdb_stream; covered by live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream reverse direction (type-21 which supersedes 15)."}
 {"id":"frankenredis-hjzc","title":"[HIGH] SCAN-family: iteration order + invalid-cursor wording + NOVALUES support delta","description":"Surfaced by live_redis_core_scan_matches_runtime: (a) SCAN/HSCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN return entries in hashbrown probe order vs upstream's dict-array order (same unspecified-semantics case as HGETALL — can canonicalize in live harness). (b) Invalid/negative cursor emits 'ERR value is not an integer or out of range' vs upstream 'ERR invalid cursor'; negative cursor should be clamped to 0 per upstream (returns empty reply). (c) COUNT 0 rejected with generic parse error vs upstream 'ERR syntax error'. (d) HSCAN/ZSCAN NOVALUES flag is a Redis 7.4 feature — we support it, vendored 7.2.4 rejects with syntax error. Fix: add scan-pair canonicalizer in live_oracle_frames_match + align fr-command cursor-parse error wording. NOVALUES cases XFAIL until vendored redis is upgraded to 7.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:20:18.972139187Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T22:52:15.818704531Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T22:52:15.816726846Z","close_reason":"Matrix live-oracle test passing 100% under STRICT mode.  {"id":"frankenredis-qhid","title":"Implement metatables (getmetatable/setmetatable) for Lua 5.1 compatibility","description":"# Lua Metatables

## What
Metatables are a key Lua 5.1 feature. Some Redis Lua scripts use them for operator overloading or custom table behavior. Currently missing from the hand-written interpreter.

## Why
While rare in typical Redis scripts, metatables are part of the Lua 5.1 spec. Their absence could break third-party Lua libraries that depend on them.

## Implementation
1. Add a metatable field to LuaTable
2. Implement getmetatable()/setmetatable() global functions
3. Implement __index, __newindex, __tostring, __eq, __lt, __le, __add, __sub, __mul, __div, __mod, __pow, __concat, __len, __call metamethods
4. Dispatch to metamethods when operations fail on the base table

## Scope
This is significant work (~200-400 lines). Prioritize __index and __tostring as they are most commonly used.

## Files
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:08.354875762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:10:45.092683154Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:10:45.092404106Z","close_reason":"Implemented getmetatable/setmetatable with real metatable storage (Option<LuaTable> in LuaTableInner). Added __index metamethod support via get_with_index() for table subscript, field access, and method calls. Supports chained __index (table→table). Added 5 tests covering get/set, __index fallback, chain, priority, and nil removal.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-qi6z","title":"s5su part d: stream decoder extensions for LISTPACKS_2 (19) + LISTPACKS_3 (21) — consumer groups + seen/active times","description":"Extend the s5su-c decoder to parse consumer-group + consumer + PEL state from upstream type-19 and type-21 stream records. Populates the groups vec on RdbValue::Stream.

Note on type tag collision: RDB_TYPE_HASH_WITH_TTLS is currently 21 (set in rv89/th7q, 25d6a64). This conflicts with upstream's RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 which is also 21. Options:
  * Option A: Shift our internal HashWithTtls to a reserved range (e.g., 100+) so upstream 21 can be our decoder-only stream path.
  * Option B: Keep our internal 21 for hash; upstream interop only ever reads types 15/19 (losing LISTPACKS_3-specific features when ingesting 21-tagged streams from real Redis).

Plan: adopt Option A. Revise th7q's type byte to 100, document the change, re-run th7q tests. Then use 21 exclusively for the stream decoder in upstream-produced files.

-{"id":"frankenredis-s5su","title":"HIGH: RDB type 15 (stream) encoding diverges from upstream — file interchange broken","description":"## Summary

Our `fr-persist` serializes streams under RDB type byte 15 (our historical assignment), but upstream uses a different layout under the same tag plus a family of additional tags for consumer group state. Project memory note `project_rdb_stream_incompatibility.md`: \"RDB type 15 stream encoding differs from Redis, breaking file interchange\".

Impact: any user who takes an upstream `dump.rdb` and boots our server on it gets a parse failure or (worse) silently-truncated stream state. Symmetrically, our RDB cannot be loaded by upstream. That violates AGENTS.md Compatibility Doctrine strict mode, which promises \"maximize observable compatibility for V1-scoped APIs\" with \"no behavior-altering repairs\".

## Surface to verify

Upstream `rdb.h` / `rdb.c` (see `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/rdb.h` and `rdb.c`):

- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS` (15) — listpack-bodied stream
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_2` (19) — adds per-consumer seen_time
- `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3` (21) — adds per-consumer active_time (7.2+)

Each shape writes:
1. radix tree: (stream-id, listpack)
2. stream metadata: length, last_id, first_id, max_deleted_id, entries_added
3. per consumer group: name, last_id, PEL size, PEL entries, consumer list
4. per consumer: name, seen_time (v2+), active_time (v3+), pending entry IDs

Our encoder should write v3 by default (matches upstream 7.2+) and decode all three.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses + /testing-fuzzing

**Conformance (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip):**
- Fixture corpus: 20 upstream-produced `.rdb` files with stream shapes covering each axis (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time).
- Round-trip gate:
  1. `fr-persist::rdb::decode(bytes)` must succeed on every fixture.
  2. Re-encode; feed to upstream `redis-server --loadrdb`, query `XINFO STREAM FULL`; canonicalize and compare to our canonicalized `XINFO STREAM FULL` post-load.
  3. Reverse: encode our in-memory stream, have upstream load it, compare stream state vector.

**Fuzzing (/testing-fuzzing, structure-aware):**
- Extend the existing `fuzz_rdb_structured.rs` with a stream-producing `arbitrary::Arbitrary` generator: random entries, groups, PELs. Invariant: for any generated shape, `decode(encode(x)) == x` AND upstream can load the encoded bytes (this last check runs gated, driven by a `REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1` env var).
- Extend `fuzz_rdb_decoder.rs` with upstream-produced corpus (minimized from the 20 fixtures above) so crashes on adversarial streams are found quickly.

## Related

- Frames our broader persistence story for streams; the `core_stream.json` conformance fixtures already exist (`crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_stream.json`, 114 KB).
- Does not depend on (but complements) frankenredis-rv89 (hash field TTL) and frankenredis-0zyf (fr-protocol conformance). All three share the upstream-fixture harvesting infra.

## Acceptance

- 20/20 upstream fixtures decode cleanly.
- Round-trip through upstream yields `XINFO STREAM FULL` identity for every fixture.
- Fuzz target logs zero crashes / zero mismatches over 1M iterations.

## Labels

fr-persist, rdb, streams, conformance-gap, file-interchange, testing-conformance-harnesses","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T03:30:14.504545661Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:13:53.323992378Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance-gap","file-interchange","fr-persist","rdb","streams","testing-conformance-harnesses"]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-v4x3","title":"CRITICAL: WrongSubcommandArity emits 'subcommand' suffix; upstream Redis 7.2.4 emits 'command' — two tests fail","description":"## Summary

`cargo test --workspace` fails on main (HEAD eb6b915) with two assertions about the wording of `WrongSubcommandArity`. Our implementation special-cases `acl | slowlog | xgroup | xinfo` so the error ends with `subcommand` instead of `command`. A live probe against the vendored `legacy_redis_code/redis` binary (version 7.2.4, also reconfirmed against 7.4.8 per prior `PINNED 2026-04-22` comments in `fr-command`) shows upstream always ends the string with `command`. Our code is the divergence.

## Failing tests

- `fr-command` lib tests:
  - `lua_eval::tests::acl_admin_subcommands_reject_from_scripts_after_validation`
    - got: `ERR wrong number of arguments for 'acl|whoami' subcommand`
    - expected: `ERR wrong number of arguments for 'acl|whoami' command`
  - `tests::wrong_subcommand_arity_formats_redis_families_with_expected_wording`
    - got: `ERR wrong number of arguments for 'acl|setuser' subcommand`
    - expected: `ERR wrong number of arguments for 'acl|setuser' command`

## Live oracle probe (redis_version 7.2.4)

```
$ redis-cli ACL WHOAMI extra
ERR wrong number of arguments for 'acl|whoami' command

$ redis-cli ACL SETUSER
ERR wrong number of arguments for 'acl|setuser' command

$ redis-cli XGROUP CREATE
ERR wrong number of arguments for 'xgroup|create' command

$ redis-cli XINFO STREAM
ERR wrong number of arguments for 'xinfo|stream' command
```

Upstream source `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/server.c:3792`:
```c
*err = sdscatprintf(*err, "wrong number of arguments for '%s' command", c->cmd->fullname);
```
and `acl.c:3033`:
```c
addReplyErrorFormat(c, "wrong number of arguments for '%s' command", cmd->fullname);
```
The suffix is unconditionally `"command"`; there is no `"subcommand"` suffix anywhere.

## Where it comes from

`crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:685-691`:
```rust
let kind = match cmd_lower.as_str() {
    "acl" | "slowlog" | "xgroup" | "xinfo" => "subcommand",
    _ => "command",
};
RespFrame::Error(format!(
    "ERR wrong number of arguments for '{}|{}' {}",
    cmd_lower, sub_lower, kind
))
```

The special-case branch fires for every subcommand of ACL/SLOWLOG/XGROUP/XINFO. This includes at least 15 `WrongSubcommandArity` call sites scattered through `fr-command` (5145..5427, 6258..10729, etc.), all of which now emit the wrong string on the wire.

Git log shows this was touched by `993bfb0 fix(command): use Redis command wording for subcommand arity` and `8f3ee5d fix(command): distinguish subcommand vs command in wrong-arity errors; FUNCTION STATS shape fixes` — the "distinguish" commit re-introduced the divergence that the earlier "use Redis wording" commit had removed.

## Suggested fix direction

Collapse the match to always emit `"command"`. The whole `kind` variable can go; the format string becomes:
```rust
format!("ERR wrong number of arguments for '{cmd_lower}|{sub_lower}' command")
```
No call-site changes needed. The existing two tests already assert the correct string.

## Labels

ci · tests · fr-command · conformance · acl · arity · upstream-divergence","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T03:25:06.444977185Z","created_by":"cc-redis","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:06:46.988465674Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:06:46.986711733Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 212bcb8 — collapsed WrongSubcommandArity suffix match to unconditional 'command' per upstream Redis 7.2.4/7.4 behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acl","arity","ci","conformance","fr-command","tests","upstream-divergence"]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-v7zc","title":"Add pub/sub cross-client integration test via TCP","description":"# Pub/Sub Cross-Client TCP Test

## What
An integration test that demonstrates pub/sub message delivery between multiple TCP clients connected to FrankenRedis.

## Why
Pub/sub delivery is tested via internal APIs (conformance fixtures) but never through actual independent TCP connections. A subscriber on one connection must receive messages published from a completely separate connection.

## Implementation

### Test 1: Basic Pub/Sub
1. Start frankenredis on random port
2. Client A (thread 1): SUBSCRIBE channel1, wait for subscription confirmation
3. Client B (thread 2): PUBLISH channel1 "hello" → verify returns 1 (one subscriber)
4. Client A: Read next message, verify ["message", "channel1", "hello"]
5. Client A: UNSUBSCRIBE channel1

### Test 2: Pattern Subscribe
1. Client A: PSUBSCRIBE "news.*"
2. Client B: PUBLISH news.sports "goal!" → returns 1
3. Client A: Verify ["pmessage", "news.*", "news.sports", "goal!"]

### Test 3: Multiple Subscribers
1. Client A: SUBSCRIBE channel1
2. Client C: SUBSCRIBE channel1
3. Client B: PUBLISH channel1 "broadcast" → returns 2
4. Both A and C receive the message

### Test 4: Unsubscribe stops delivery
1. Client A: SUBSCRIBE channel1
2. Client B: PUBLISH channel1 "msg1" → A receives
3. Client A: UNSUBSCRIBE channel1
4. Client B: PUBLISH channel1 "msg2" → returns 0 (no subscribers)

## Logging
- Log all pub/sub operations with timestamps
- Log message received events: "[ClientA] RECEIVED: message channel1 hello"
- Log subscriber counts on PUBLISH

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/pubsub_e2e.rs (new)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.136076437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:05:42.368395054Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:05:42.368142626Z","close_reason":"Added 3 pub/sub E2E tests to tcp_e2e_test.rs: tcp_pubsub_basic_cross_client_delivery (subscribe/publish/unsubscribe lifecycle), tcp_pubsub_multiple_subscribers (broadcast to 2 clients), tcp_pubsub_pattern_subscribe (PSUBSCRIBE with pmessage delivery). All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankenredis-v8s5","title":"HIGH: DUMP/RESTORE use frankenredis-private encoding — MIGRATE interop with upstream Redis impossible","description":"## Summary

`Store::dump_key` at `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:9433-9511` emits a payload using a **frankenredis-internal** type-byte scheme and length encoding, not the upstream RDB format. The doc comment even undersells it:

```
/// Format: [type_byte][payload][8-byte TTL-ms or 0][2-byte CRC placeholder]
```

(Actual format: 8-byte TTL, 1-byte version tag = 10, 8-byte CRC64 — so the doc comment is stale: we do write a real 8-byte CRC64 using the Redis polynomial, not a 2-byte placeholder. That part is fine.)

The deeper problem: the type bytes are flat {0=string, 1=list, 2=set, 4=hash, 5=sortedset, 15=stream} with raw-length encoding and raw-bytes payloads. Upstream RDB uses encoding-specific type tags (`RDB_TYPE_STRING`=0 with LZF or raw subtag, `RDB_TYPE_SET_INTSET`=11, `RDB_TYPE_HASH_ZIPLIST`=13, `RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST_2`=18, `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3`=21, etc.). Our encoding is upstream-incompatible across EVERY type.

Consequence: a client that `DUMP`s a key from FrankenRedis and `RESTORE`s into upstream Redis gets `ERR DUMP payload version or checksum are wrong` because the version byte 10 doesn't match upstream's version-field semantic (upstream gates on `rdbVersion == supported_range`, which our `10` superficially satisfies — but the payload parser fails on unknown type bytes and \"wrong\" length encoding even before the version check). Symmetrically, upstream DUMP output can't be restored into FrankenRedis — `restore_key` at lib.rs:9547 only recognizes our type bytes {0,1,2,4,5,15}.

**Impact on `MIGRATE`:** MIGRATE uses DUMP + RESTORE as its wire format. A `MIGRATE` from FrankenRedis to a real Redis node fails; the reverse too. Clustered operations are therefore fundamentally broken at the data-transfer layer, even before the cluster-admin bead (`frankenredis-jsr7`).

## Distinguishing from frankenredis-s5su

`frankenredis-s5su` covers **RDB file format** for streams specifically (type 15 inside .rdb files). This bead covers **DUMP/RESTORE wire format** for ALL types (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, streams) — a different binary blob that flows inside a single RESP BulkString over the network, used by MIGRATE and by direct DUMP/RESTORE commands.

There is some overlap in infrastructure (both need to speak upstream's type-tag matrix), but the file-format ingestion from disk and the wire-format ingestion from MIGRATE take different code paths in upstream Redis too, and we should track them separately.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip)

- Corpus: for each upstream-supported Redis type + each upstream-supported encoding variant, capture `DUMP` output from vendored `redis-server` and store under `crates/fr-store/tests/fixtures/dump_roundtrip/`.
- Gate 1: our `restore_key` must accept every fixture without error.
- Gate 2: after RESTORE into our store, re-DUMP; feed that DUMP bytes to upstream `redis-server` and ask it to RESTORE; then query the key on upstream; canonicalize and compare to the original upstream state.
- Gate 3: the reverse direction — seed our store, DUMP, feed to upstream, read the key on upstream, canonicalize, compare to our in-memory value.

## Applying /testing-fuzzing

- `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dump_restore.rs` exists but is self-referential (roundtrip inside FrankenRedis only). Retarget it as **differential**: `arbitrary::Arbitrary` → generate a Value, DUMP via ours, RESTORE via upstream (if present), verify roundtrip through both directions. Today this fuzzer passes because it never checks upstream; once real, it surfaces every type where our encoding diverges.

## Fix scope

- `fr-store::dump_key`: switch to upstream RDB type tags + encoding-aware serialization (reuse `fr-persist::rdb::*` helpers; they already encode full RDB records).
- `fr-store::restore_key`: dispatch on upstream type tags; reject our legacy types {1,2,4,5,15} with a one-release deprecation warning OR (better, given AGENTS.md \"no backwards-compatibility shims\") hard-remove them and regenerate any persisted .rdb fixtures in-tree.
- DUMP version tag must follow the upstream-compatible supported range (`RDB_VERSION` 11 for Redis 7.2.x, 12 for 7.4.x). Pick the one the vendored `legacy_redis_code/redis` targets and pin.

## Acceptance

- `DUMP ... → RESTORE ...` succeeds byte-identically across (FrankenRedis → FrankenRedis), (FrankenRedis → upstream), (upstream → FrankenRedis) for every upstream-observable type/encoding pair.
- `MIGRATE` from FrankenRedis to upstream moves a key successfully (observed via `GET`/`HGETALL`/etc. on the destination).
- `fuzz_dump_restore` retargeted differential-mode runs 1M iterations against the vendored oracle with zero mismatches outside documented XFAILs.

## Labels

+{"id":"frankenredis-v8s5","title":"HIGH: DUMP/RESTORE use frankenredis-private encoding — MIGRATE interop with upstream Redis impossible","description":"## Summary

`Store::dump_key` at `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:9433-9511` emits a payload using a **frankenredis-internal** type-byte scheme and length encoding, not the upstream RDB format. The doc comment even undersells it:

```
/// Format: [type_byte][payload][8-byte TTL-ms or 0][2-byte CRC placeholder]
```

(Actual format: 8-byte TTL, 1-byte version tag = 10, 8-byte CRC64 — so the doc comment is stale: we do write a real 8-byte CRC64 using the Redis polynomial, not a 2-byte placeholder. That part is fine.)

The deeper problem: the type bytes are flat {0=string, 1=list, 2=set, 4=hash, 5=sortedset, 15=stream} with raw-length encoding and raw-bytes payloads. Upstream RDB uses encoding-specific type tags (`RDB_TYPE_STRING`=0 with LZF or raw subtag, `RDB_TYPE_SET_INTSET`=11, `RDB_TYPE_HASH_ZIPLIST`=13, `RDB_TYPE_LIST_QUICKLIST_2`=18, `RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3`=21, etc.). Our encoding is upstream-incompatible across EVERY type.

Consequence: a client that `DUMP`s a key from FrankenRedis and `RESTORE`s into upstream Redis gets `ERR DUMP payload version or checksum are wrong` because the version byte 10 doesn't match upstream's version-field semantic (upstream gates on `rdbVersion == supported_range`, which our `10` superficially satisfies — but the payload parser fails on unknown type bytes and \"wrong\" length encoding even before the version check). Symmetrically, upstream DUMP output can't be restored into FrankenRedis — `restore_key` at lib.rs:9547 only recognizes our type bytes {0,1,2,4,5,15}.

**Impact on `MIGRATE`:** MIGRATE uses DUMP + RESTORE as its wire format. A `MIGRATE` from FrankenRedis to a real Redis node fails; the reverse too. Clustered operations are therefore fundamentally broken at the data-transfer layer, even before the cluster-admin bead (`frankenredis-jsr7`).

## Distinguishing from frankenredis-s5su

`frankenredis-s5su` covers **RDB file format** for streams specifically (type 15 inside .rdb files). This bead covers **DUMP/RESTORE wire format** for ALL types (strings, lists, sets, hashes, sorted sets, streams) — a different binary blob that flows inside a single RESP BulkString over the network, used by MIGRATE and by direct DUMP/RESTORE commands.

There is some overlap in infrastructure (both need to speak upstream's type-tag matrix), but the file-format ingestion from disk and the wire-format ingestion from MIGRATE take different code paths in upstream Redis too, and we should track them separately.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses (Pattern 3: cross-impl round-trip)

- Corpus: for each upstream-supported Redis type + each upstream-supported encoding variant, capture `DUMP` output from vendored `redis-server` and store under `crates/fr-store/tests/fixtures/dump_roundtrip/`.
- Gate 1: our `restore_key` must accept every fixture without error.
- Gate 2: after RESTORE into our store, re-DUMP; feed that DUMP bytes to upstream `redis-server` and ask it to RESTORE; then query the key on upstream; canonicalize and compare to the original upstream state.
- Gate 3: the reverse direction — seed our store, DUMP, feed to upstream, read the key on upstream, canonicalize, compare to our in-memory value.

## Applying /testing-fuzzing

- `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dump_restore.rs` exists but is self-referential (roundtrip inside FrankenRedis only). Retarget it as **differential**: `arbitrary::Arbitrary` → generate a Value, DUMP via ours, RESTORE via upstream (if present), verify roundtrip through both directions. Today this fuzzer passes because it never checks upstream; once real, it surfaces every type where our encoding diverges.

## Fix scope

- `fr-store::dump_key`: switch to upstream RDB type tags + encoding-aware serialization (reuse `fr-persist::rdb::*` helpers; they already encode full RDB records).
- `fr-store::restore_key`: dispatch on upstream type tags; reject our legacy types {1,2,4,5,15} with a one-release deprecation warning OR (better, given AGENTS.md \"no backwards-compatibility shims\") hard-remove them and regenerate any persisted .rdb fixtures in-tree.
- DUMP version tag must follow the upstream-compatible supported range (`RDB_VERSION` 11 for Redis 7.2.x, 12 for 7.4.x). Pick the one the vendored `legacy_redis_code/redis` targets and pin.

## Acceptance

- `MIGRATE` from FrankenRedis to upstream moves a key successfully (observed via `GET`/`HGETALL`/etc. on the destination).
- `fuzz_dump_restore` retargeted differential-mode runs 1M iterations against the vendored oracle with zero mismatches outside documented XFAILs.
